title.es: MLE Falla con Pocos Datos: El Caso ZYX
title.en: MLE Fails with Small Data: The ZYX Case

summary_es: Este modulo demuestra el problema central de MLE con datasets pequenos usando el caso ZYX: una empresa con 3 trimestres de earnings beats donde MLE estima P(beat)=100% con certeza total. Se construye el posterior Beta-Binomial con priors uniforme y esceptico, mostrando que PML da 80% con incertidumbre honesta (HDI 38-99%). Se implementa actualizacion secuencial con 8 trimestres incluyendo un 'miss' en Q6, mostrando que MLE cae bruscamente 17pp mientras PML cae suavemente 11pp. Se generan distribuciones predictivas para los proximos 4 trimestres, comparando la sobreconfianza de MLE con el realismo de PML. Se analiza la sensibilidad al prior con pocos datos y su desaparicion con muchos.
summary_en: This module demonstrates MLE's core problem with small datasets using the ZYX case: a company with 3 quarters of earnings beats where MLE estimates P(beat)=100% with total certainty. The Beta-Binomial posterior is built with uniform and skeptical priors, showing PML gives 80% with honest uncertainty (HDI 38-99%). Sequential updating over 8 quarters including a Q6 'miss' shows MLE drops sharply by 17pp while PML drops smoothly by 11pp. Predictive distributions for the next 4 quarters compare MLE's overconfidence with PML's realism. Prior sensitivity with small data and its disappearance with large data is analyzed.
learning_objectives_es: Demostrar que MLE produce p=1 con 3/3 datos (absurdo estadistico)
Construir el posterior Beta-Binomial como alternativa con incertidumbre
Implementar actualizacion secuencial y cuantificar el impacto de un miss
Comparar distribuciones predictivas MLE vs PML para decisiones futuras
Analizar sensibilidad al prior con pocos datos
learning_objectives_en: Demonstrate that MLE produces p=1 with 3/3 data (statistical absurdity)
Build the Beta-Binomial posterior as an alternative with uncertainty
Implement sequential updating and quantify the impact of a miss
Compare MLE vs PML predictive distributions for future decisions
Analyze prior sensitivity with small datasets

parameters.name: MLE Small Sample Absurdity
parameters.origin_phrase: MLE estimates P(beat)=100% with only 3 data points

parameters.name: Prior as Protection
parameters.origin_phrase: Even a uniform prior reduces MLE's absurd 100% to a realistic 80%

parameters.name: Robustness to Outliers
parameters.origin_phrase: PML absorbs a miss smoothly (11pp) while MLE drops sharply (17pp)
